title: Recover Super Admin Principal ID Access

requirements.text: Initialize the Super Admin role in the backend by setting the principal ID 'fiawz-svdcq-fsplo-srina-bia3r-kdxu5-c4tcj-e7bkm-w3gex-cvpvm-2qe' as the Super Admin principal in the main actor's initialization logic

quotes: recover my super admin principal ID my super admin ID is fiawz-svdcq-fsplo-srina-bia3r-kdxu5-c4tcj-e7bkm-w3gex-cvpvm-2qe

acceptanceCriteria: The principal ID 'fiawz-svdcq-fsplo-srina-bia3r-kdxu5-c4tcj-e7bkm-w3gex-cvpvm-2qe' is hardcoded as the Super Admin principal in backend/main.mo
When the user authenticates with Internet Identity using this principal, their role is recognized as Super Admin
The Super Admin has full access to all admin features including User Management, Analytics Dashboard, and Storage Monitor
The Super Admin principal assignment persists across canister upgrades

requirements.text: Add a Super Admin recovery mechanism in the AdminDashboardPage that allows the principal 'fiawz-svdcq-fsplo-srina-bia3r-kdxu5-c4tcj-e7bkm-w3gex-cvpvm-2qe' to reinitialize Super Admin access if it was lost

quotes: recover my super admin principal ID

acceptanceCriteria: The AdminDashboardPage displays a recovery button or automatic recovery flow when the authenticated principal matches the hardcoded Super Admin ID but lacks Super Admin permissions
Clicking the recovery mechanism calls a backend method to restore Super Admin role
After successful recovery, the user's role updates to Super Admin without requiring re-authentication
The recovery UI provides clear feedback on success or failure

constraints: Preserve all existing backend state variables and data structures
Do not change the authentication flow or Internet Identity integration
Maintain compatibility with existing role-based access control system
nonGoals: Reverting to Version 193 (user should start new conversation for revert operation)
Fixing YouTube embed issues in BNHS Hymn section
Addressing other broken features from Version 200
